properties.description: DDH 4  trees along the left side of the house.

properties.tree_description:
See site map Reason for Removal:OtherComments:A metal fence post is embedded within the structural root plate. There is very early, incipient growth of
bark into portions of the adjacent chain-link fence. Tree protection fencing is present at the base, and site
conditions indicate prior disturbance consistent with demolition activity (late 2019–early 2020). These
conditions present uncertainty and concern regarding prior protection and potential impacts within the
critical root zone; the extent of pre-construction protection/inspection remains unknown.
		
		
			Tree number:2Species:Dogwood Tree Size (DBH):8Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:See site map Reason for Removal:OtherComments:This tree has a lean greater than 20 degrees. It has a mechanical wound at the base, and the structural
root plate is deformed in position directly above a failing CMU block retaining wall.
		
		
			Tree number:3Species:Water Oak Tree Size (DBH):26Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:See site map Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Sounding of the basal trunk suggests the presence of concealed internal decay. The structural root plate
is deformed where it abuts the existing driveway. An adjacent decayed stump from a previous tree
removal is present within the structural root plate, indicating a history of root disturbance. Portions of the
chain-link fence have girdled into the structural root plate.
